reviews.review_description: Its so hard to play without controers
Is there a free perk or something to those who purchased it now that its free? Yes, im salty about that

developer_response.body:
This change in strategy is misfortunate and we're sorry about this. Learners who purchased the application will have lifetime access, even when we reintroduce pricing later in 2024. Those who have had free access during this time period will lose access once the App price is added again.  You're invited to our live AMA session this Thursday the 18th 7:30 (MST) where we'll directly address this issue and why we've made this decision, along with other topics. 

Here's the link: https://youtube.com/live/b8K1AKMIuZA?feature=share

reviews.review_title: The best clinical training app on quest.
reviews.review_description:
Very great for fine tuning your clinical examination skills. 
Using it on the Quest 3 I see there are a few bugs when it comes to palpation. For example when I try to palpate different pulses the gripping function never seems to go to the right region and also doesn't seem to register that I have palpated that region on the teach function.
Also hands and stethoscope are able go through the patient which also adds to the difficulty of testing specific points on the patient.

It would be cool if we could get some lab results sheets for patients or ECGs and be tested on them. 

But I see the direction and can't wait to see the progress and overall there is so much good about this app so 5 Stars!

developer_response.body:
Hi Dr. Lemon! 
Thanks a lot for the review. We have all those features and debugging list in the roadmaps, so please be patient, we are doing our best to improve the game. With the time we hope to have a patient with several interactions to solve the lack of patients in the real world. 
Good luck on your training!

reviews.review_description:
I'm giving this a 5 star, cause it's a good concept. There's a few issues that I found with this app. 

1) Frame rate dips, and this results in straining of my eyes. I had to take the headset off after a few minutes. 

2) Up close to any subject, it is very clear. But as soon as you move like a meter away, everything becomes really blurred. (There is a lot of shimmer on edges.) 

Maybe the developer could work on anti aliasing. 

3) I haven't used the app much, so I'm not sure if the application has this, but it might be a lot easier to have an index of the various stations like on a wall or on the pause menu. Where you can easily select any station.

4) I understand that the developer is trying to simulate the hospital as much as possible, but walking around a big room, or even teleporting gets a bit too tedious. 

It may be better if the room was much smaller, and all the instruments are closer to you. 

I'll update this list after I have my second session :P
---------------

Please don't misunderstand this review as hate! I genuinely see the potential in this app! And I'm honestly really excited to use it.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review! We really appreciate the kind words, and we absolutely love getting feedback. Med Valley is a constantly improving work-in-progress, with a small and nimble team. Thanks for playing, thanks for the feedback, and please keep it coming!
Have a nice day!

reviews.review_description: In this app you can perform basic physical exams and perform cases. It is early days and features are currently limited. The app is still unpublished as of today so expectations do need to be lowered however I am happy to give 5 stars in advance. Also for the price you won't get anything better.  The team is extremely responsive to feedback and are constantly working to improve this app and make it more comprehensive for features and cases. I look forward to upcoming versions of this app. I don't doubt that medvalley become part of the future for medical training!

developer_response.body: Hey SandyEg0, Your words are really appreciated to give us strength to continue, thanks for understand that this is in an early stage, more features coming soon! and new patients are being developed, Have fun!

reviews.review_title: A way to go, but heading the right way!
reviews.review_description: I love checking for the little updates each week. I have a feeling it's not going to be fleshed out enough to really help with my upcoming osce. Go team!

developer_response.body: Hi! treeml, thanks a lot for your feedback, We are doing our best to add more features asap, so please patience, if this won't help for your upcoming osce, surely it will for the future ones, when we have the customizable ones, good luck!

reviews.review_description:
The game is really impressive! You can do histories and physical exams in VR. There are some limitations but they have released updates at least every other week with more OSCEs, bug fixes, and new physical exam interactions. 

Also, they just added the ability to learn about certain exams and physical exam interactions so I can see how it can be really helpful to new medical learners. 

Give it a couple months and we might not need medical schools anymore.
